Title: Splash screen, footer, and the broken robot
Summary: The intro animation, the credit line at the bottom, and what happens when something throws.
Body:
## Splash screen

On every fresh mount (real refresh, first load, or a back-forward cache restore), the app shows a splash. App icon, BLEEPFORGE title, version, and a real progress bar wired to two checkpoints — the server health probe and the preferences fetch + theme apply. There's no fixed duration: on fast hardware the splash flashes through in under a second; on a slow boot it waits.

Flavor lines under the bar advance per checkpoint (one of 15 "polishing bleeps" / "calibrating scavengers" picks). Once both checkpoints land, a sticky READY message replaces the flavor and the CONTINUE button reveals — that pause exists so even a sub-second boot still feels like an event rather than a flicker. Click CONTINUE (or wait for the 10-second fallback if a checkpoint never resolves and you want to push through anyway) to dismiss.

The current URL is preserved across the splash because the router doesn't re-mount — F5 on `/quests` goes splash → CONTINUE → `/quests`. Popout windows skip the splash entirely; they're focused subviews, not full sessions.

## Footer

Every non-popout page carries a footer at the bottom with the authorship credit. On short pages the footer is pinned to the viewport bottom; on long pages it scrolls into view at the end. It never occludes content. The same credit line is also pinned to the bottom of the splash overlay so it greets you on both ends.

## /boom

There is an intentional easter egg route at `/boom` that throws synchronously during render. It exists to verify the [error boundary](/help/under-the-hood/error-boundary) by tripping it on demand. Useful when you change boundary chrome and want to see the fallback without finding a real bug.
